task_completion.description: Verify the basic details of the new aircraft 'Falcon Eye 3' are correctly entered.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a Django admin page (either a success message, list view, or edit form) showing an Aircraft record with the Name 'Falcon Eye 3', Flight Controller ID 'FE3CTRL334455', and Status set to 'Active'.

task_completion.description: Verify the aircraft 'Falcon Eye 3' is linked to the correct operator and manufacturer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Aircraft record details showing the Operator as 'A.J. August Photography' and Manufacturer as 'Aerobridge Drone Company'.

task_completion.description: Verify the AircraftDetail record for 'Falcon Eye 3' is properly registered.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an AircraftDetail record associated with 'Falcon Eye 3' showing the Registration mark 'IND/UP/2024/003' and a visual indication that it is marked as registered (e.g., a checked box or 'True' status).

task_completion.description: Verify the FlightOperation record is created with the correct primary entities.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a FlightOperation record with the Name 'Falcon Eye 3 Maiden Flight', linked to the Aircraft 'Falcon Eye 3', Operator 'A.J. August Photography', and Pilot 'Rakesh Kankipati'.

task_completion.description: Verify the FlightOperation record includes the correct plan, purpose, type, and datetimes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the FlightOperation record showing Flight Plan 'Flight Plan A', Purpose 'photographing', Operation type 'VLOS', and valid entries for both start and end datetimes.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the web browser to interact with the Django admin interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show a web browser navigating the Aerobridge Django admin pages at http://localhost:8000/admin/, rather than terminal commands like curl or python scripts executing API calls.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually created the records in the system.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for standard Django admin success messages (e.g., 'The aircraft was added successfully.') and standard Django admin UI elements, ensuring the agent didn't just type the expected values into a text editor.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not use a database client to insert records directly.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal windows should not show SQL INSERT commands, sqlite3, or psql shell prompts being used to bypass the web interface.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task is performed in a local Django admin interface at http://localhost:8000/admin/. The agent is expected to create synthetic test records with the following exact values: Aircraft Name: 'Falcon Eye 3', Flight Controller ID: 'FE3CTRL334455', Registration Mark: 'IND/UP/2024/003', Operation Name: 'Falcon Eye 3 Maiden Flight', Operator: 'A.J. August Photography', Pilot: 'Rakesh Kankipati', and Manufacturer: 'Aerobridge Drone Company'. These are fictional entities created specifically for this test.
